stories.title: Food assistance slashed for hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees trapped in Bangladesh camps
stories.description: Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees struggling to survive in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps will see their food assistance slashed starting on Wednesday, raising alarm throughout the increasingly desperate community.

stories.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
stories.description: As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.

stories.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
stories.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.

stories.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
stories.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

stories.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
stories.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

stories.title: Trump hints at an end to military action in Iran, saying U.S. will leave in 2-3 weeks
stories.description: Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
stories.description: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
stories.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US
stories.description: Seizing the stockpile would be one of the "most complicated special operations in history", a former defence official tells the BBC.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: As US pursues 'maximalist demands', Iran relies on four decades of asymmetric warfare
stories.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Ali Vaez, Director of the Iran Project & International Crisis Group Senior Advisor. He examines the evolving dynamics of the confrontation between the US, Israel, and Iran. Military escalation risks triggering systemic regional and global conse...

stories.title: Middle East live: Houthis claim missile attack on Israel as Trump says Iran war to end within weeks
stories.description: Yemen's Houthis on Wednesday claimed a missile attack against Israel they said was launched jointly with their backer Iran and Lebanon's Hezbollah – the third such attack since they entered the war. US President Donald Trump is set to make an address about the Iran war, a day aft...

stories.title: EU faces 'largest oil supply disruption in history', energy expert says
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Francesco Sassi, Assistant Professor at the University of Oslo and energy geopolitics expert, says that the Iran war has provoked "the largest oil supply disruption in history" adding that "what is really concerning is that national gover...

stories.title: US fuel prices surge past $4 a gallon
stories.description: The average price of regular gasoline in the United States topped $4 dollars a gallon for the first time since 2022, as the Iran war continues to disrupt global energy supply. This as inflation in the Eurozone rose past the ECB's target to 2.5% in March. Meanwhile, Egyptian shops...

stories.title: US journalist kidnapped in Baghdad by suspected Iranian-backed armed group
stories.description: A woman US journalist who was kidnapped in Iraq's capital city by a suspected Iranian-backed Iraqi armed group, US officials said on Tuesday. Advocacy groups identified the missing woman as Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist based in Rome who has covered several wars in the...

stories.title: No, Iranian hackers didn’t leak a video of the FBI's Kash Patel dancing
stories.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has been the subject of social media buzz this week, after his private emails were hacked by an Iranian-linked group. There's also a viral Bollywood dance video being falsely linked to the incident, despite no connection to Patel, as Vedika Bahl clears up ...

stories.title: Gulf allies privately make the case to Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
stories.description: Gulf states, led by Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, are urging Trump to continue prosecuting the war against Iran, arguing that Tehran hasn't been weakened enough by the bombing campaign, according to U.S., Gulf and Israeli officials. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with ...

stories.title: Iran attacks cause fire in Kuwait, Bahrain; kill man in UAE
stories.description: Shrapnel from an Iranian drone interception kills a Bangladeshi national in the UAE's Fujairah city.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 33 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: Trump says US could leave Iran war in 2-3 weeks without a deal; Iran’s FM Araghchi says he has no faith in talks.

stories.title: Al-Sharaa says Syria to stay out of war on Iran unless attacked
stories.description: '14 years of war is enough for Syria': Ahmed al-Sharaa says Syria will remain outside the US-Israeli war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump tells allies ‘get your own oil’, says Iran war could end in 2-3 weeks
stories.description: Under pressure as domestic petrol prices soar, US president says no deal needed to end war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran war live: ‘Missile from Iran’ hits oil tanker off Qatar’s coast
stories.description: One of three missiles launched from Iran hit an oil tanker off Qatar's coast, no injuries reported.

stories.title: Trump says US will be leaving Iran in two to three weeks
stories.description: Speaking to reporters in the Oval Office, US President Donald Trump says US could leave Iran “in two or three weeks."

stories.title: Pope Leo urges Trump to end war on Iran
stories.description: Pope Leo XIV has urged US President Donald Trump to find an “off-ramp” from the US and Israel’s war on Iran.

stories.title: After Strait of Hormuz opens, turmoil would still last months, analysts say
stories.description: Effective shutdown of key waterway could lead to permanent changes in how shipping industry does business.

stories.title: US exempts Gulf of Mexico oil drillers from endangered species protections
stories.description: The Trump administration says protections would diminish US energy competitiveness amid the ongoing war on Iran.

stories.title: EU warns oil and gas prices won’t quickly return to normal after Iran war
stories.description: The EU’s energy commissioner has warned oil and gas prices will not return to normal any time soon.

stories.title: China’s FM Wang Yi appreciates Pakistan’s active diplomatic efforts in promoting Middle East peace: FO
stories.description: China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi has conveyed Beijing’s appreciation for Pakistan’s “active diplomatic role” and efforts towards ending the Middle East conflict, the Foreign Office (FO) said on Wednesday. Wang made the remarks during his meeting with Deputy Prime Minister and For...

stories.title: Buy jet fuel from US or get your own, Trump rails against allies
stories.description: • Hegseth claims next few days to be ‘decisive’, says conflict to intensify if deal not made• Pezeshkian says Iran wants to end war but not without guarantees; Araghchi confirms messages exchanged with US, denies talks• Iranian missiles injure nine in Tel Aviv; US companies in re...

stories.title: Arab states lost $186bn in first month of war, UN official says
stories.description: AMMAN/SINGAPORE: The war sparked by the joint US-Israeli attack on Iran and which has spread across the Middle East has cost Arab countries $186 billion, a top UN official said on Tuesday, as he called for an immediate halt to the fighting. “We hope the fighting will stop tomorro...

stories.title: Pentagon denies Hegseth’s broker wanted to invest in arms before war
stories.description: WASHINGTON: The United States Department of Defence has called for the withdrawal of a newspaper report alleging that a broker linked to Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth sought to make a significant investment in weapons companies ahead of the war with Iran, Al Jazeera reported. Pe...

stories.title: King Charles to pay state visit to US as UK seeks to mollify Trump
stories.description: King Charles will make a state visit to the United States in late April, Buckingham Palace said on Tuesday, a high-profile trip that the British government hopes will help to repair relations with President Donald Trump, damaged by the war on Iran. Charles and his wife Queen Cami...

stories.title: Pakistan, China issue 5-point initiative for restoring peace in Middle East
stories.description: Pakistan and China on Tuesday issued a five-point initiative for restoring peace and stability in the Gulf and the Middle East amid the US-Israeli war on Iran, in which they called on the immediate cessation of hostilities and starting peace talks as soon as possible. The develop...

stories.title: Pakistan, seven other countries condemn Israeli curbs on Muslim and Christian worship in occupied Jerusalem
stories.description: Pakistan, along with seven other countries, condemned the restrictions imposed by Israel on the freedom of worship for Muslims and Christians in occupied Jerusalem, the Foreign Office (FO) said on Tuesday. Since Israel and the United States started a war with Iran on Feb 28, Isra...

stories.title: ‘Diplomacy takes time’: Pak envoy reaffirms Islamabad’s commitment to Iran-US peace amid complex situation
stories.description: Pakistan’s Ambassador to the US Rizwan Saeed Sheikh has reaffirmed Islamabad’s commitment to facilitating dialogue between the United States and Iran, even as regional tensions intensify. In an interview broadcast on Tuesday, Ambassador Sheikh told Fox News that Pakistan is “comm...

stories.title: Pak-China talks in Beijing to ‘refine’ framework for possible US-Iran dialogue
stories.description: https://www.dawn.com/news/1986999

stories.title: Israel passes law making death penalty default sentence for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks
stories.description: Israel’s parliament approved a bill on Monday that would allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks, a move that has been criticised as discriminatory and immediately drew a court challenge. Sixty-two lawmakers, including Prime Minister Ben...

stories.title: ‘All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth’: IMF says Iran war is dimming outlook for many economies
stories.description: The war in the Middle East has caused serious disruption to the economies of frontline countries, and is dimming the outlook for many economies that had just started to recover from previous crises, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) warned on Monday. In a blog published by th...

stories.title: Dar hints at US, Iran talks as quad pushes peace off-ramp
stories.description: • Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt back efforts to create conditions for Washington-Tehran structured negotiations• FMs explore ways for permanent end to hostilities, insist war is ‘not in favour of anyone’• Deputy PM to leave for Beijing to meet Chinese foreign minister tomorrow ...

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Iranian FM says 'not offered anything in response' to U.S. plan; Houthis claim third missile attack against Israel
stories.description: UAE prepares to help U.S. open Strait of Hormuz, says report; Trump says war could end in 'two to three weeks'

stories.title: Trump says Iran war could end in ‘two weeks, maybe three,’ to address U.S.
stories.description: The White House announced meanwhile that Donald Trump would give an address to the nation at 9:00 pm EST Wednesday (6.30 a.m. IST Thursday) "to provide an important update on Iran."

stories.title: Iran’s Parliament commission approves Hormuz toll plan: state TV
stories.description: A member of Parliament’s security commission reported that the plan includes “financial arrangements and rial toll systems”, “implementing Iran’s sovereign role”, and cooperation with Oman across the Strait

stories.title: Kuwait says Iran drone attack sets airport fuel tanks ablaze, no casualties
stories.description: The attack caused significant damage to airport fuel tanks ‌belonging to the ​Kuwait Aviation Fuelling Company

stories.title: Iran Guards say will target U.S. tech firms if more leaders killed
stories.description: "We advise the employees of these institutions to immediately leave their workplaces to preserve their lives," says Iran’s Revolutionary Guards

stories.title: Systematically crushing Iran, forging alliances with important countries in West Asia: Netanyahu
stories.description: Mr. Netanyahu stressed that “sooner or later” the Iranian regime will fall, while Israel creates “new alliances with important countries in the region” against the shared threat emanating from Tehran

stories.title: Iran-Israel war highlights: Trump says U.S. to leave Iran 'very soon,' deal or not
stories.description: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth claimed that talks are making progress; China and Pakistan put forward a five-point peace proposal to end the West Asia war

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
